import struct

buildid_hex = "4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"
buildid = bytes.fromhex(buildid_hex)

# ELF64 header for aarch64
e_ident = b'\x7fELF' + b'\x02' + b'\x01' + b'\x01' + b'\x00' + b'\x00'*8
ehdr = e_ident
ehdr += struct.pack('<H', 3)       # e_type: ET_DYN (shared object)
ehdr += struct.pack('<H', 183)     # e_machine: EM_AARCH64
ehdr += struct.pack('<I', 1)       # e_version
ehdr += struct.pack('<Q', 0)       # e_entry
ehdr += struct.pack('<Q', 64)      # e_phoff (right after ehdr)
ehdr += struct.pack('<Q', 0)       # e_shoff
ehdr += struct.pack('<I', 0)       # e_flags
ehdr += struct.pack('<H', 64)      # e_ehsize
ehdr += struct.pack('<H', 56)      # e_phentsize
ehdr += struct.pack('<H', 1)       # e_phnum
ehdr += struct.pack('<H', 64)      # e_shentsize
ehdr += struct.pack('<H', 0)       # e_shnum
ehdr += struct.pack('<H', 0)       # e_shstrndx

# Build the note section content
note_name = b'GNU\x00'
note_desc = buildid
note = struct.pack('<III', len(note_name), len(note_desc), 3)  # NT_GNU_BUILD_ID=3
note += note_name
note += note_desc

# PT_NOTE program header
note_offset = 64 + 56  # after ehdr + 1 phdr
phdr = struct.pack('<I', 4)         # p_type: PT_NOTE
phdr += struct.pack('<I', 4)        # p_flags
phdr += struct.pack('<Q', note_offset) # p_offset
phdr += struct.pack('<Q', 0)        # p_vaddr
phdr += struct.pack('<Q', 0)        # p_paddr
phdr += struct.pack('<Q', len(note)) # p_filesz
phdr += struct.pack('<Q', len(note)) # p_memsz
phdr += struct.pack('<Q', 4)        # p_align

with open('libcoreclr.so', 'wb') as f:
    f.write(ehdr + phdr + note)

print(f"Created ELF with build-id {buildid_hex}")

3. **A core dump** (not just a tombstone) — `dotnet-symbol` can fetch symbols automatically from a core dump.
